• Conversational Agent Foundations
Build your first AI chatbot with no-code tools, then graduate to calling LLM APIs through LangChain with safe wrappers, prompt management and tool calling so the agent can take real actions.

issues.Content: Page lists 6 project cards (Code Review, Customer Support, Candidate Assessment, Customer & Competitor Analysis, Investment Recommendations, Sales Enablement). Product note lists 3 primary capstone use cases plus 2 additional use cases (5 total).
issues.Fix Suggested:
Replace the entire content with the below one:
1. Wellbeing Coach – Your Personalized 5-Minute Mental Booster

An AI-powered application that understands user input and context to deliver personalized wellness activities, providing quick and effective interventions through intelligent recommendations and adaptive responses.
Tags: PYTHON, FASTAPI, API DEVELOPMENT, LLM INTEGRATION, ASYNC, TESTING

2. Real Estate Assistant – AI-Powered Home Search Companion

A conversational AI agent that helps users discover, compare, and evaluate homes through personalized, context-aware interactions, integrating tools, memory, and workflows to deliver relevant property insights.
Tags: GENAI, CONVERSATIONAL AI, AGENT WORKFLOWS, TOOL CALLING, CLOUD DEPLOYMENT

3. MedLearn Assistant – AI-Powered Medical Education Companion

An agentic RAG system that processes medical textbooks and multi-modal content to deliver accurate, explainable answers for learning and concept clarification.
Tags: GENAI, RAG, MULTI-MODAL, DATABASE, AI SECURITY

4. Autonomous Social Media Content Studio – Multi-Agent Publishing System

A multi-agent system that transforms a single idea into platform-specific content, using specialized agents, review loops, and coordinated workflows to deliver consistent, publish-ready outputs.
Tag: GENAI, MULTI-AGENT SYSTEMS, AGENT WORKFLOWS, AUTONOMOUS AGENTS, PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION

5. Multi-Agent Retail Policy Intelligence System

A crew of agents that interprets retail policies, validates decisions against business rules, and provides consistent, explainable outcomes for policy-driven scenarios across operations.
Tag: GENAI, RAG, DATABASE, AI SECURITY, PERFORMANCE METRICS

6. Multi-Agent Support Resolution System with Human in the Loop

A hierarchical workflow of agents with Support Specialist and Resolution QA roles that diagnose issues using documentation and historical data, escalating unresolved or high-risk cases to human experts.
Tag: GENAI, RAG,DATABASE, AI SECURITY, PERFORMANCE METRICS

issues.Fix Suggested:
Add six project cards:
1. Fitness Tracker Database - Design a fitness tracker database managing workouts, health data, subscriptions, and personalized progress tracking insights.
2. Online Bookstore Database — Create a bookstore database to manage books, orders, payments, inventory, and customer engagement insights.
3. EcoRide – Create a Java ride-sharing simulator with role-based menus for drivers and riders including booking and validation.
4. Online Polling System with Real-Time Analytics – Build a real-time polling system with vote processing, duplicate prevention, instant analytics, and rollback capabilities.
5. EventEase – Develop a Java event management app with venue scheduling, attendee registration, and edge-case handling for bookings.
6. FarmConnect – Design a farmer-to-buyer marketplace for listing, ordering, and tracking fresh produce with inventory management.

issues.Content: Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing a professional Capstone Project on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.
issues.Gap / Issue: Callout uses the term 'Capstone Project' which does not exist as a course in this program;
issues.Fix Suggested: Replace with: 'Boost Your Career Visibility — Showcasing your professional Course-End Projects on your LinkedIn increases recruiter interest significantly. Build production-ready work that speaks for itself.'

issues.Content: Page outcomes: 1) Design and manage relational databases using SQL... 2) Develop Java applications using core programming concepts... 3) Apply data structures and algorithms... 4) Write optimized and maintainable code using Java Collections, Lambdas, and Stream APIs. 5) Test and validate Java programs using unit testing and debugging techniques. 6) Build structured software solutions...
issues.Fix Suggested: Add a closing outcome bullet: 'Be job-ready for the entry-level Java Developer role by combining technical skills with professional best practices and the right engineering mindset.'

issues.Gap / Issue: assessment and project is missing without which we cannot show 4 weeks in the schedule
issues.Fix Suggested:
Under Curriculum Add:
"11. Project – Build Solution (Week 3) – 8 Hours
Apply all skills to build a complete application
Build and refine a Java application by applying core programming concepts such as logic building, methods, and clean coding practices to create a functional solution.

ACTIVITY
Project development using IntelliJ; Version control using Git (commit and push code)

12. Course-End Assessment (Week 4)- 4 Hours
Validate knowledge and skills gained
Assess understanding of core Java concepts and the ability to apply them effectively through structured assessment.

ACTIVITY
Concept-based assignments and evaluation

13. Project Presentation (Week 4)-8Hours
Showcase the final working application
Demonstrate the completed project by explaining the logic, approach, and key decisions taken during development.

ACTIVITY
Presenting project implementation; Explaining code flow and decisions

issues.Gap / Issue: SQL and ChatGPT are not covered but are present in the list
issues.Fix Suggested: Display only Java and GIT

issues.Gap / Issue: Project Titles are missing can be included with a small discription
issues.Fix Suggested:
"Add six project cards:
1. DonateConnect – A Community Donation and Distribution System Connect donors with NGOs to redistribute usable items like clothes, books, and electronics to people in need.
2. EcoRide – A Simple Ride-Sharing System	Build a ride-sharing system connecting drivers and riders with booking, validation, and capacity management features.
3. EventEase – Simple Event & Venue Management System	Create an event management system for organizing venues, scheduling events, and enabling attendee discovery and registration.
4. FarmConnect – A Farmer-to-Market Supply Chain	Design a farmer-to-buyer marketplace for listing, ordering, and tracking fresh produce with inventory management.
5. GreenPay – A Smart Waste Collection & Reward System	Develop a waste collection platform rewarding citizens with cashback for responsible disposal and sustainable waste management.
6. PetPal – Adoption Management for Pet Shelters	Build a pet shelter system for listing animals, managing adoption applications, and matching pets with adopters.
"
